_notes: Hand-labeled eval set. Labels were drafted from well-documented sources; REVIEW EACH LABEL YOURSELF before trusting eval numbers — the whole point of a golden set is that a human personally vouches for every answer. difficulty: easy = unambiguous with abundant sources, hard = requires nuance or has misleading surface signals.

claims.claim: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at sea level.
The Eiffel Tower is located in Paris, France.
Mount Everest is the tallest mountain on Earth above sea level.
The human heart has four chambers.
Australia is both a country and a continent.
Amazon was founded by Jeff Bezos in 1994.
The speed of light in a vacuum is approximately 299,792 kilometers per second.
Properly stored honey essentially never spoils.
The Great Barrier Reef is the world's largest coral reef system.
Tokyo is the capital of Japan.
The Python programming language was created by Guido van Rossum.
The 2024 Summer Olympics were held in Paris.
Sharks existed on Earth before trees did.
The Great Wall of China is visible from the Moon with the naked eye.
Humans use only 10 percent of their brains.
Albert Einstein failed mathematics in school.
Sydney is the capital of Australia.
Goldfish have a memory span of only three seconds.
Napoleon Bonaparte was unusually short for a man of his era.
Lightning never strikes the same place twice.
Mount Kilimanjaro is the tallest mountain in the world.
Vaccines cause autism.
The Sun orbits the Earth.
Vikings wore horned helmets in battle.
Thomas Edison was the first person to invent an electric light bulb.
Bats are blind.
Tesla was founded by Elon Musk in 2003.
claims.notes: Founded in 2003 (true) by Eberhard and Tarpenning; Musk joined in 2004 as an investor/chairman (so 'founded by Musk' is false).

claims.claim: Christopher Columbus reached the Americas in 1492, proving for the first time that the Earth was round.
claims.notes: 1492 voyage: true. 'Proving the Earth was round': false — sphericity was long established.

claims.claim: The Berlin Wall fell in 1989, marking the official end of World War II.
claims.notes: Fell 1989: true. WWII ended 1945: the second half is false.

claims.claim: Mount Everest, located in Japan, is the tallest mountain above sea level.
claims.notes: Tallest: true. Located in Japan: false (Nepal/China border).

claims.claim: Albert Einstein won the 1921 Nobel Prize in Physics for his theory of relativity.
claims.notes: Won the 1921 prize: true. For relativity: false — it was for the photoelectric effect.

claims.claim: The Amazon rainforest is the largest tropical rainforest on Earth and lies entirely within Brazil.
claims.notes: Largest: true. Entirely within Brazil: false (spans 9 countries; ~60% in Brazil).

claims.claim: The Titanic sank in 1912 on its maiden voyage after hitting an iceberg, killing everyone on board.
claims.notes: 1912/maiden voyage/iceberg: true. Everyone died: false (~700 survived).

claims.claim: There are exactly 3 trillion fish in the Pacific Ocean right now.
claims.notes: No source could verify an exact live count.

claims.claim: Extraterrestrials visited Earth during the construction of the Egyptian pyramids.
claims.notes: No credible evidence either way; fringe claim.

claims.claim: Coffee is the best beverage in the world.
claims.notes: Pure opinion — not a checkable factual claim.

claims.claim: A butterfly flapping its wings in Brazil caused Hurricane Katrina.
claims.notes: Chaos-theory metaphor; untestable as a causal claim.

claims.claim: Shakespeare's plays were secretly written by Francis Bacon.
claims.notes: Fringe authorship theory; mainstream scholarship rejects it but definitive proof either way is absent — a defensible alternative label is 'false'.

claims.claim: Humanity will establish a permanent colony on Mars by 2050.
claims.notes: Future prediction — cannot be verified today.

claims.claim: Satoshi Nakamoto, the creator of Bitcoin, is actually a group of five people.
claims.notes: Identity remains unknown; the specific 'five people' claim has no evidence.
